data.keywords: ["air quality stations", "AQI", "air quality descriptions", "China", "monitoring stations", "ST series dataset", "geographic space", "grid", "temporal granularity", "Gaussian model", "PyKridge library", "Kriging interpolation", "DBSCAN", "MinPts", "window size"]

evaluation.reason: The evidence provides context about the distribution of monitoring stations and describes the AQI scale, which is useful but lacks direct answers or paths for further investigation related to a specific research question.
evaluation.insight: Figure 5 shows the spatial distribution of 448 air quality monitoring stations across China and maps AQI values to air quality descriptions, providing a foundation for further spatial-temporal analyses.

nodes.label: figure_1:the study a...
nodes.full_title: figure_1:the study area and its typical urban agglomerations. abbreviations: scb, sichuan basin; cc, central china; yrd, yangtze river delta; bth, beijing-tianjin-hebei.

data.keywords: ["Sichuan Basin", "Central China", "Yangtze River Delta", "Beijing-Tianjin-Hebei", "urban agglomerations", "China", "grid resolution", "horizontal grid spacing", "vertical levels", "simulation domain", "data assimilation (DA)", "bias correction (BC)", "PM2.5 predictions", "WRF-Chem", "GSI (Gridpoint Statistical Interpolation)", "deep-learning-based BC", "RMSEs"]

evaluation.reason: The evidence directly pertains to the impact of combined statistical methods on PM2.5 predictions, valuable for designing a predictive model or understanding regional pollution dynamics.
evaluation.insight: Figure 1 presents a map of China highlighting urban areas experiencing severe pollution, and provides quantitative evidence on improvement in PM2.5 prediction with DA and BC methods.

nodes.label: figure_6:case study ...
nodes.full_title: figure_6:case study of air quality in china. (a) volumestcube provides the overview of air quality with  $\lambda = 0$ . (b) the visual pattern becomes clearer with a larger  $\lambda = 150$ . (c) the temporal variation of air quality in xinjiang and (d) in the bth region were revealed via volume highlight. (e, e1, and e2) the air pollution during the spring festival was generally below the moderate level. (f, f1, and f2) the air pollution caused by the dust storm from late march to april impacted a wide spatial range. (g) the air pollution caused by the dust storm in late may impacted a smaller spatial range.

data.keywords: ["air quality", "China", "VolumeSTCube", "temporal variation", "Xinjiang", "BTH region", "volume highlight", "Spring Festival", "dust storm", "air pollution"]

evaluation.reason: While this figure illustrates visualization efficacy, it does not offer new scientific insights beyond visualization capabilities or specific findings tied to research questions.
evaluation.insight: VolumeSTCube is used to visualize air quality data in China, showcasing spatio-temporal impacts of events like dust storms with varied lambda values for clarity.

nodes.label: figure_7:case study ...
nodes.full_title: figure_7:case study of air quality in china. (a) air quality data is visualized with the surface rendering in volumestcube, where (a1 and a2) two propagation processes of air pollution can be identified. (b) after clicking (a1), the user can obtain this view. (c) the air pollution propagated from the loess plateau to the north china plain. (d) after clicking (a2), the user can obtain this view. (e) the air pollution propagated from the north china plain to the yangtze river delta.

data.keywords: ["air quality data", "VolumeSTCube", "air pollution", "Loess Plateau", "North China Plain", "Yangtze River Delta"]

evaluation.reason: The evidence relates more to evaluation of visualization tools rather than providing substantive scientific data or findings pertinent to pollution dynamics.
evaluation.insight: VolumeSTCube significantly improves accuracy and reduces time for identifying air pollution propagation compared to baseline methods.

nodes.full_title: figure_7:temperature in china. (a) storyline with (a1) the shade mapping. (b) upper right corner of (a), where (b1) periodicity and (b2) drops are observed. (c) colored result of (b). (d) the spatial distribution of colored st series in (c). (e) line charts and (f) trend motifs of (c1), where obvious drops are observed in (e1, f1).

evaluation.insight: 图中介绍了 GeoChron 系统的多层次可视化能力，通过故事线和 EvoLens 展示空间和时间数据分析，帮助全面理解复杂现象。

nodes.label: figure_1:Our impleme...
nodes.full_title: figure_1:Our implementation of Thakur and Hanson's method [14], later used as a baseline in the comparative user study.

data.keywords: ["Thakur and Hanson's method", "Space-Time Cube", "ST series", "baseline", "hotspot", "occlusion", "VolumeSTCube"]

evaluation.reason: Provides context on baseline visualization techniques but does not offer further exploration paths.
evaluation.insight: This figure presents Thakur and Hanson's method for visualizing spatiotemporal series data as a baseline, highlighting its limitations such as occlusion and difficulty in identifying hotspots.

nodes.label: figure_2:Pipeline of...
nodes.full_title: figure_2:Pipeline of VolumeSTCube. (A) The spatial time (ST) series are placed in a 3D space following the space-time cube. (B) After interpolation and smoothing, the ST series are transformed as a space-time volume. (C) The volume is visualized with volume rendering and surface rendering, and is equipped with temporal slicing, spatial filter, and voxel cluster-based interactions, forming VolumeSTCube.

data.keywords: ["VolumeSTCube", "Space-Time Cube", "ST series", "interpolation", "smoothing", "space-time volume", "volume rendering", "surface rendering", "temporal slicing", "spatial filter", "voxel cluster", "voxel"]

evaluation.reason: Illustrates the core methodology of VolumeSTCube with clear transformation and visualization techniques, suitable for deeper investigation.
evaluation.insight: The figure visually explains the VolumeSTCube methodology, demonstrating the transformation of discrete spatial time series data into a continuous volumetric representation through interpolation and rendering techniques for effective analysis.

nodes.label: figure_3:Visual desi...
nodes.full_title: figure_3:Visual designs of VolumeSTCube. (A) Space-time volume visualization with volume rendering and contextual information. (B and C) A propagation process of air pollution is visualized with volume rendering and surface rendering, respectively.

data.keywords: ["VolumeSTCube", "Space-time volume visualization", "volume rendering", "surface rendering", "air pollution", "propagation process", "spatiotemporal domains", "visual occlusion", "depth ambiguity", "spatial time series data"]

evaluation.reason: Effectively showcases VolumeSTCube's application in visualizing complex spatiotemporal phenomena, offering substantial value over prior methodologies.
evaluation.insight: The figure demonstrates the visualization of air pollution propagation using VolumeSTCube's rendering techniques, addressing visual occlusion and providing detailed patterns for analysis.

nodes.label: figure_4:Interaction...
nodes.full_title: figure_4:Interactions in VolumeSTCube. (A) Volume Slicing. (B) Volume Spotlight. (C) Voxel cluster-based selection.

data.keywords: ["VolumeSTCube", "Volume Slicing", "Volume Spotlight", "Voxel cluster-based selection", "ST series", "space-time volume", "spatiotemporal patterns"]

evaluation.reason: Highlights interaction capabilities within VolumeSTCube but lacks direct links to further exploration or deeper insights.
evaluation.insight: The figure demonstrates VolumeSTCube's interactive features enabling users to explore spatial and temporal data through slicing, spotlighting, and voxel cluster selection.

nodes.label: figure_8:Results of ...
nodes.full_title: figure_8:Results of user study. The results for VolumeSTCube and the baseline are represented by soft blue and soft orange, respectively. The bottom section categorizes the 18 questions into three groups based on their spatial, temporal, and spatiotemporal perspectives. The middle section displays the response times for each question with jittered dot plots, where horizontal markers denote medians. The top section shows the correctness of the answers. Black rectangles highlight instances where either the difference of the correctness is more than 25% or the response time is significantly different. No instances were found where the baseline obviously outperforms VolumeSTCube.

data.keywords: ["VolumeSTCube", "baseline", "user study", "response times", "correctness", "spatial perspective", "temporal perspective", "spatiotemporal perspectives", "Wilcoxon test"]

evaluation.reason: Offers valuable insights into the comparative performance of VolumeSTCube and baseline methods, backed by statistical tests, suggesting strong evidence for its effectiveness.
evaluation.insight: The results of the user study show that VolumeSTCube significantly outperforms the baseline in both correctness and response time across spatial, temporal, and spatiotemporal questions.

nodes.label: figure_2:flow charts...
nodes.full_title: figure_2:flow charts of the four different experiments. case 1: wrf-chem; case 2: wrf-chem_da; case 3: wrf-chem_bc; case 4: wrf-chem_da_bc.

data.keywords: ["WRF-Chem", "WRF-Chem_DA", "WRF-Chem_BC", "WRF-Chem_DA_BC", "GSI", "OBS", "DA", "BC", "MDS-UNet"]

evaluation.reason: 该图为不同的试验提供了方法论基础，文中详细分析了它们对PM2.5预测性能的影响，是进一步研究的关键信息。
evaluation.insight: 该图概述了四种试验方法（WRF-Chem，WRF-Chem_DA，WRF-Chem_BC和WRF-Chem_DA_BC），并展示数据同化（DA）与偏差校正（BC）如何应用于提高PM2.5预测。这提供了深入比较这些方法在不同条件下性能的基础。

evaluation.reason: 图表虽然提供了一种布局优化的视觉展示，但缺乏与主要研究问题的直接相关性，信息价值较低。
evaluation.insight: 这幅图展示布局优化技术，如松散对齐和会话过滤，旨在提高长篇布局的可读性和空间效率。

nodes.label: figure_1:flowchart s...
nodes.full_title: figure_1:flowchart showing the procedures of the constraining analysis in the current study. the colors indicate the modeled relationships (blue), satellite observations (orange), in situ relationships (purple), field measurements of emission factors (efs) for the primary bba (gray), and constrained estimates (red). in particular, the satellite observation is labeled with a subscript of “0,” and constrained values are labeled with a subscript of “c.” f₁ and f₂ show the linear regressions developed from the aerocom models. f₃ indicates the observed relationship from (24). abbreviations/notations are defined as follows: ssa (single - scattering albedo), ae (angstrom exponent), precip (precipitation), mac (mass absorption coefficient), τ (lifetime), and e (emission).

data.keywords: ["constraining analysis", "modeled relationships", "satellite observations", "in situ relationships", "emission factors (EFs)", "primary BBA", "constrained estimates", "SSA", "AE", "Precip", "MAC", "lifetime", "E", "AeroCom models", "POLDER-GRASP", "GPCP", "AAOD", "SOA", "BC", "OA"]

evaluation.reason: 虽然该图未直接关联到当前研究问题，但它介绍了一种新的分析方法，可以为未来相关性研究提供背景知识。
evaluation.insight: 流程图展示了一种新的紧急约束方法论，将气溶胶光学厚度分解为特定因子（如排放，生命周期，MAC），使用模型关系和卫星观测，旨在改善计算的准确性。
